name: sn-scriptsync
description: sn-scriptsync for VS Code. (Provided by SN Utils B.V.)

commands.title: sn-scriptsync: Retrieve current version from Instance
sn-scriptsync: Load/Refresh artifacts from scope (include empty)
sn-scriptsync: Load instance tablenames and properties for IntelliSense
sn-scriptsync: Execute Backgroundscipt (.js files in /background folder only)
sn-scriptsync: Create a new artifact in ServiceNow
sn-scriptsync: Take Screenshot of ServiceNow Page

editor/context.title: Load instance tablenames and properties for IntelliSense
Execute Background Script in Instance
editor/context.when: sn-scriptsync.serverRunning && sn-scriptsync.showContextMenu && editorLangId == javascript && resourceDirname =~ /background/ && resourceFilename =~ /^[^_]/

sn-scriptsync.path.description: Scripts folder (Extension will auto activate when folder path ends with this name)

sn-scriptsync.externalChanges.monitorFil.description: Monitor external file changes (AI agents/git/tools) and show them in the Pending Saves queue. Turn off to fully disable monitoring.

sn-scriptsync.externalChanges.syncDelay.description: Seconds to wait before auto-syncing monitored external changes. Default is 0 (monitor-only). Use Sync Now or manual save to push changes, or set a value > 0 to enable auto-sync.

sn-scriptsync.createArtifacts.enabled.description: Agent API permission (1/4 — create): Allow creating new records in ServiceNow from files/Agent API. Disable to block all create-record operations.

sn-scriptsync.restRequest.enabled.description: Agent API permission (2/4 — write): Allow the generic rest_request passthrough to perform write methods (POST/PUT/PATCH). GET is always allowed; DELETE additionally requires Delete Records.

sn-scriptsync.deleteRecords.enabled.description: Agent API permission (3/4 — delete): Allow the Agent API to delete records in ServiceNow (delete_record, and DELETE via rest_request). Off by default — enable only when you trust the connected agent.

sn-scriptsync.backgroundScripts.enabled.description: Agent API permission (4/4 — run scripts): Allow the Agent API to run background scripts on the instance (run_background_script, and the cascade used by delete_application). Off by default — this executes arbitrary server-side code as the connected user.

sn-scriptsync.browserDebugger.enabled.description: Agent API (beta): Allow the Agent API to drive the connected ServiceNow tab through the Chrome DevTools Protocol browser debugger — network/console capture, full-page screenshots, and native dialog handling (start_network_capture, capture_full_page, set_dialog_handler, etc.). Off by default: attaching the debugger shows Chrome's 'started debugging this browser' banner, and it's a SN Utils Pro browser feature. Enable to opt into the beta.

sn-scriptsync.agentApi.fileFallback.description: Keep the legacy file-based Agent API (agent/requests/*.json) active alongside the HTTP Agent API. Disable once all your AI agents use the HTTP endpoint documented in agentinstructions.md.

sn-scriptsync.agentInstructions.autoUpda.description: On start, add and refresh the managed sn-scriptsync reference block inside your own agent instruction files (CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursorrules / .windsurfrules / .clinerules / .github/copilot-instructions.md). Turn off if you maintain your own agent instructions and don't want sn-scriptsync touching those files. NOTE: agentinstructions.md and the agentrules/skills folder are always kept up to date either way, so you can still reference them on demand (e.g. @agentinstructions.md) — opting out only stops the block being injected into your files.

sn-scriptsync.showContextMenu.description: Show sn-scriptsync commands in the editor context menu. Can be configured per language (e.g., disable for markdown files).

sn-scriptsync.debugLogging.description: Write verbose diagnostics to debug.log in the workspace root. Useful for troubleshooting; can generate a lot of output when syncDelay > 0.

viewsWelcome.contents: Please load scope via right click context menu.
